message:
test: pin sigtimedwait conformance via differential harness

Adds tests/conformance_diff_sigwait.rs covering sigtimedwait:
- empty-set + zero-timeout returns -1 EAGAIN on both
- block SIGUSR1 + raise + sigtimedwait dequeues with signo

sigwaitinfo and sigsuspend are static-link-only — both block
indefinitely without a pending signal, and kill+sigwaitinfo races
are unreliable under the multi-threaded test runner.

3 tests pass, 0 divergences against glibc.

Bead: bd-sigw1

 {"id":"bd-cmiv","title":"EPIC: Implement real DNS resolution for getaddrinfo","description":"getaddrinfo() currently only resolves:
1. Numeric IP addresses (127.0.0.1, ::1)
2. Hostnames found in /etc/hosts

It does NOT perform actual DNS queries. This means programs like wget, curl (for non-localhost URLs), dig, nslookup, and any network client that resolves hostnames will fail.

OPTIONS:
A) Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric, non-/etc/hosts hostnames
B) Implement a minimal DNS stub resolver (send UDP query to nameserver from /etc/resolv.conf)
C) Both: try our resolver first, fall back to host

Option A is fastest to implement and most reliable. Option B gives us full independence from host libc.

ALREADY DONE: /etc/hosts lookup works, numeric IP parsing works, freeaddrinfo correctly frees our Box-allocated structures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.603558212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.403160236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.402959135Z","close_reason":"Already implemented. getaddrinfo has a full native DNS stub resolver: udp_dns_query() sends A/AAAA queries to /etc/resolv.conf nameservers via SYS_socket/SYS_sendto/SYS_recvfrom with configurable timeout/retry. ResolverConfig parses nameservers, search domains, ndots. Resolution chain: numeric -> /etc/hosts -> DNS -> repair fallback. 46 resolv tests pass. Previously verified when closing bd-zh1y.7 and bd-1y7.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cmiv.1","title":"Add host libc delegation for DNS-requiring getaddrinfo calls","description":"When getaddrinfo receives a hostname that:
1. Is NOT a numeric IP address
2. Is NOT found in /etc/hosts

Delegate to the host libc's getaddrinfo via our ELF resolver. The host's getaddrinfo will perform the actual DNS query.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. resolve_host_symbol_raw("getaddrinfo") to get host's function pointer
2. Call host_getaddrinfo(node, service, hints, res) 
3. Return the host's result directly (the addrinfo structs are heap-allocated by the host)
4. In freeaddrinfo, detect whether the addrinfo was allocated by us (Box) or host (malloc) and free accordingly

TRICKY PART: freeaddrinfo must distinguish our Box-allocated addrinfos from host-malloc-allocated ones. Could use a sentinel field or check if the pointer is in our Box allocation range.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.450087855Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.448613314Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cmiv.1","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-cnf1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc sysconf+pathconf+confstr diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 4 functions: sysconf (12 common names cross-checked), pathconf (/, _PC_NAME_MAX/_PC_PATH_MAX), fpathconf (/dev/null), confstr (_CS_PATH size+content, _CS_GNU_LIBC_VERSION zero-match). Discovered DISC-SYSCONF-001: fl returns POSIX min for _SC_CHILD_MAX/_SC_STREAM_MAX while glibc queries live rlimit. Discovered DISC-SYSCONF-002: fl returns POSIX min for _PC_LINK_MAX (127) while glibc queries actual filesystem (65000 on ext4). Both documented; bd-cnf2 opened. 8 tests, 0 substantive divergences.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; documented divergences logged.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","sysconf"]}
+{"id":"bd-cnf2","title":"FIX: sysconf/pathconf should query runtime limits, not return POSIX minimums (DISC-SYSCONF-001/002)","description":"Discovered by sysconf diff harness. fl::sysconf(_SC_CHILD_MAX) returns 32768 (the POSIX minimum) while glibc returns the actual RLIMIT_NPROC (typically 883226). fl::sysconf(_SC_STREAM_MAX) returns -1 while glibc returns 16. fl::pathconf('/', _PC_LINK_MAX) returns 127 while glibc queries the filesystem (65000 on ext4). Fix: query getrlimit for CHILD_MAX, return FOPEN_MAX for STREAM_MAX, statfs the path for filesystem-specific PC_* limits.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","conformance","sysconf"]}
 {"id":"bd-cnxe","title":"syscall: eventfd_write invalid fd should preserve EBADF even for forbidden all-ones payload","description":"Host glibc returns EBADF for eventfd_write(-1, UINT64_MAX), so the invalid-fd contract dominates the forbidden payload. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:39:25.750825423Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T01:18:55.383949478Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T01:18:55.381776786Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-cs9w","title":"Left-Right Sync for complex metadata reads (section 14.3)","description":"Implement Left-Right synchronization for wait-free reads of complex metadata structures in FrankenLibC. Left-Right maintains two copies of the data structure (left and right). Readers always access one copy wait-free (no locks, no atomics on the read path). Writers update the inactive copy, then toggle the active pointer, then wait for readers to drain from the old copy before updating it too. Compared to RCU (bd-3aof), Left-Right provides: (a) Wait-free reads (not just lock-free). (b) Works for non-RCU-friendly data structures (those requiring consistent snapshots of multiple fields). Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Allocator configuration — the allocator's configuration (size-class table, arena count, thread-to-arena mapping) is a complex multi-field structure that must be read consistently. Left-Right provides atomic snapshot reads without locks. (2) TSM policy table — the TSM's decision policy (state machine transitions, threshold parameters) may be updated at runtime (e.g., when switching from strict to hardened mode). Left-Right ensures readers always see a consistent policy snapshot. (3) Symbol dispatch table — the IFUNC-like dispatch table mapping symbols to implementations. Updated when CPU features change (e.g., AVX-512 becomes available after cpuid check). Implementation: LeftRight<T> wrapper with read() -> &T (wait-free) and write(|t: &mut T| ...) (serialized, may block briefly).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.3 of graveyard. Left-Right from Ramalhete & Correia (2015). Wait-free reads with consistent snapshots for multi-field structures.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- LeftRight<T: Clone>: maintains two copies (left: Box<T>, right: Box<T>) with version_index: AtomicBool.
- read() -> ReadGuard<T>: returns reference to active copy. No atomic RMW on read path (single atomic load of version_index).
- ReadGuard registers in per-reader arrival/departure counters (2 counters, one per version).
- write(|t: &mut T| ...) -> (): acquires writer mutex, updates inactive copy, toggles version_index, waits for readers on old version to drain, updates other copy.
- Reader drain: spin on reader counter for old version until zero. Bounded wait if readers are well-behaved.
- T: Clone required to synchronize the two copies after toggle.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. LeftRight<T> compiles and passes miri for basic read/write cycle.
2. Read path is wait-free: no CAS, no RMW (verified by cargo asm inspection).
3. Consistent snapshot: multi-field read always sees consistent state (test with 2-field struct updated atomically).
4. 16-reader + 1-writer stress test: 1M operations, TSan clean.
5. Read latency <3ns at p99 (compare with RCU at <2ns and RwLock at ~15ns).
6. Writer latency <1us including reader drain wait at p99 under 16-reader load.
7. No starvation: writer completes within bounded time even with continuous readers.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: left_right, operation=read, version, reader_count) per read (disabled in release).
- tracing::debug!(target: left_right, operation=write, version_toggled, drain_wait_ns) per write.
- tracing::warn!(target: left_right, slow_drain, drain_wait_ns, remaining_readers) if drain exceeds 100us.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:32.674331698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T18:34:45.507096513Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T18:34:45.506818727Z","close_reason":"Implemented LeftRight synchronization in frankenlibc-membrane with cached readers, drain coordination, diagnostics, and 11 focused tests. Verified with rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane left_right -- --nocapture, rch exec cargo check --workspace --all-targets, and rch exec cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets -- -D warnings. Full-workspace clippy/fmt remain blocked by unrelated pre-existing ABI-file issues in the dirty worktree.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cs9w","depends_on_id":"bd-3aof","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-mjon","title":"bd-yos subtask: Thread bootstrap trampoline finalization for pthread_create","description":"Background:
- Thread bootstrap is a core blocker for both callthrough removal and TLS key APIs.

Goal:
- Finalize clone/trampoline startup path for pthread_create with explicit invariants and ABI boundaries.

Deliverables:
1) Thread start trampoline contract (entry args, return handling, cleanup path).
2) Parent/child synchronization at startup.
3) Error path behavior and errno propagation.

Acceptance Criteria:
- pthread_create success/failure semantics match declared support matrix behavior.
- No hidden host-libc dependency in startup path for covered mode/profile.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for bootstrap components and error branches.
- Structured logs: trace_id, thread_id, parent_id, start_state, exit_state, errno, timing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonForge","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.822186049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:27.139407950Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:27.139340033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","threading"],"comments":[{"id":1123,"issue_id":"bd-mjon","author":"CrimsonForge","text":"COMPLETED: Thread bootstrap trampoline implementation.

Files modified:
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/raw.rs: Added clone_thread_asm (inline asm trampoline for Linux clone syscall)
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs: Added SYS_GETTID, SYS_SET_TID_ADDRESS, sys_gettid, sys_exit_thread, sys_clone_thread
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs: Complete native thread creation via clone (ThreadHandle, create_thread, join_thread, detach_thread, self_tid)
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mod.rs: Updated re-exports

Quality gates:
- cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-core: PASS
- cargo check -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets: PASS
- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets -- -D warnings: PASS
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::thread: 6/6 PASS

Tests:
- create_and_join_thread_returns_value
- child_thread_can_write_shared_memory
- multiple_threads_created_and_joined
- detach_thread_does_not_error
- join_null_handle_returns_einval
- gettid_returns_positive

Architecture:
- Uses CLONE_VM|CLONE_FS|CLONE_FILES|CLONE_SIGHAND|CLONE_THREAD|CLONE_SYSVSEM|CLONE_SETTLS|CLONE_PARENT_SETTID|CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID
- Child stack: [fn_ptr][arg] layout, trampoline pops both, calls fn_ptr(arg), SYS_exit on return
- Join uses futex-wait on TID (kernel clears via CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID and wakes waiters)
- Parent/child sync via startup futex (ensures args consumed before create returns)
- 2MiB stack with 4KiB guard page via mmap","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mk07a","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc ctype.h diff matrix","description":"Differential conformance harness shipped in commit 631b5100 (crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/conformance_diff_ctype.rs).

Coverage:
- 12 classifiers (isalpha/isdigit/isalnum/isspace/isupper/islower/isprint/ispunct/isxdigit/isblank/iscntrl/isgraph)
- 2 transformers (toupper/tolower)
- Sweep: EOF + every byte 0..=255 (257 inputs)
- 3598 differential calls vs glibc reference
- 0 divergences observed

Result: ctype.h family is conformant against glibc in the C/POSIX locale.

Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_conformance_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test conformance_diff_ctype","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped in commit 631b5100; 0 divergences against glibc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential"]}

+{"id":"bd-mq2","title":"FIX: fl::mq_open returns -1 even when glibc succeeds (DISC-MQ-001)","description":"Discovered by mqueue.h diff harness. fl::mq_open(name, O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_RDWR, 0o600, &attr) returns -1 on a host where the same call via glibc succeeds. fl::mq_open uses 'mut args: ...' for varargs to extract mode + attr; suspect the variadic arg-extraction is reading the wrong type or offset. Affects: unistd_abi.rs:mq_open. Fix: verify args.arg::<libc::mode_t>() then args.arg::<*const c_void>() reads match Rust calling convention; consider non-variadic public symbols __mq_open_2 path or static C trampoline.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T15:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T15:45:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","conformance","mqueue","variadic"]}
 {"id":"bd-mrit","title":"syscall: move_mount both NULL pathnames should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"On the current host, unprivileged move_mount(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0) fails with EPERM. Add ABI regression coverage so this degenerate privilege-path behavior is documented and frozen.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:44:13.522012612Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:16:28.449203657Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:16:28.446521058Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mtj","title":"EPIC: E2E Reality Gate (build + LD_PRELOAD smoke + ABI surface)","description":"Critique mapping: #1.

Goal: make glibc_rust indisputably real by proving it builds and runs on real binaries.

Deliverables:
- CI/local gate: cargo fmt/check/clippy/test + cdylib build.
- ABI surface reports: exported symbol list + versioning + support matrix.
- LD_PRELOAD smoke harness for strict+hardened.

Acceptance:
- One command runs the full gate and returns non-zero on any drift/regression.
- Failures produce a deterministic crash bundle (backtrace + evidence snapshot).

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:06.684806153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:07.346791885Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:07.346754184Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","critique","e2e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-2cr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-2p0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-3ex","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-3jh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-6yd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Discovered DISC-SYSCONF-002: fl returns POSIX min for _PC_LINK_MAX (127) while glibc queries actual filesystem (65000 on ext4). Both documented; bd-cnf2 opened. 8 tests, 0 substantive divergences.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; documented divergences logged.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","sysconf"]}
-{"id":"bd-cnf2","title":"FIX: sysconf/pathconf should query runtime limits, not return POSIX minimums (DISC-SYSCONF-001/002)","description":"Discovered by sysconf diff harness. fl::sysconf(_SC_CHILD_MAX) returns 32768 (the POSIX minimum) while glibc returns the actual RLIMIT_NPROC (typically 883226). fl::sysconf(_SC_STREAM_MAX) returns -1 while glibc returns 16. fl::pathconf('/', _PC_LINK_MAX) returns 127 while glibc queries the filesystem (65000 on ext4). Fix: query getrlimit for CHILD_MAX, return FOPEN_MAX for STREAM_MAX, statfs the path for filesystem-specific PC_* limits.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","sysconf","conformance"]}
-{"id":"bd-mq1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc mqueue.h diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 5 functions: mq_unlink (ENOENT edge), mq_close (invalid-fd edge), libc-baseline mq_open+mq_send+mq_receive sanity, fl::mq_open documented divergence (DISC-MQ-001). 5 tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T15:45:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T15:45:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T15:45:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; DISC-MQ-001 documented; bd-mq2 opened for fl::mq_open variadic fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","mqueue"]}
-{"id":"bd-mq2","title":"FIX: fl::mq_open returns -1 even when glibc succeeds (DISC-MQ-001)","description":"Discovered by mqueue.h diff harness. fl::mq_open(name, O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_RDWR, 0o600, &attr) returns -1 on a host where the same call via glibc succeeds. fl::mq_open uses 'mut args: ...' for varargs to extract mode + attr; suspect the variadic arg-extraction is reading the wrong type or offset. Affects: unistd_abi.rs:mq_open. Fix: verify args.arg::<libc::mode_t>() then args.arg::<*const c_void>() reads match Rust calling convention; consider non-variadic public symbols __mq_open_2 path or static C trampoline.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T15:45:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T15:45:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","mqueue","conformance","variadic"]}
+{"id":"bd-sigw1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc sigtimedwait diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 1 actively-tested function: sigtimedwait (empty-set zero-timeout returns -1 EAGAIN, pending SIGUSR1 dequeues with signo). sigwaitinfo and sigsuspend are static-link-only because they block indefinitely without a pending signal and the multi-threaded test runner makes kill+sigwaitinfo races unreliable. 3 tests pass, 0 divergences against glibc.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:00:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T16:00:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T16:00:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","sigwait","signal"]}
